content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
September 7, 1989 
RIta Walters 
Los Angeles School Board Member 
P.O. Box 3307 
Los Angeles, CA 
90051 
Dear Ms. Walters: 
Re: Your Request for Informal 
Assistantce Our File No. 1-89-417 
This is in reply to your letter requesting advice concerning 
your reporting obligations under the conflict of interest 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l 
QUESTION 
May you accept a gift of a $100 travelers check from the 
Council of Black Administrators of the Los Angeles Unified School 
District? 
CONCLUSION 
You may accept the $100 travelers check. 
However, you are 
required to report this gift on your statement of Economic 
Interests (Form 730). 
FACTS 
You attended a function sponsored by the Council of Black 
Administrators of Los Angeles Unified School District. 
As a token 
of their appreciation for your leadership, the organization gave 
you a $100 travelers check. 
This gift was not in connection with 
a speech or presentation on your part. 
1 
. 
Government Code section 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322-5660 


Rita Walters 
Page Two 
ANALYSIS 
As a member of the Board of Education for Los Angeles County, 
you are required to report gifts of $50 or more from a single 
source on your annual statement of economic interests. 
section 
82028 defines gift as " ... any payment to the extent that 
consideration of equal or greater value is not received .... " 
Since the gift you received was not in connection with a 
speech, article or published work on a subject relating to the 
governmental process, the gift would not come under the gift and 
honorarium limitation in Section 85400. 
section 85400 provides 
that "no elected officeholder shall accept any gift or honorarium 
for any speec~, article, or published work on a subject relating 
to the governmental process from any single source which is in 
excess of one thousand dollars ($1,000), in any calendar year, 
except reimbursement for actual travel expenses and reasonable 
sUbsistence in connection therewith." 
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please 
conta9t me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By: Colleen McGee 
Political Reform Consultant 


BOARD OF EDUCATION 
CITY OF LOS ANGELES 
P.o. BOX 3307 / LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90051 
TEL. NO. (213) 625-6389 
July 11, 1989 
Falr PoliLical Practices Commission 
428 fiJI! street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Re: 
Request for Advice Regarding the Acceptance of 
a Gift from the Council of Black Administrators 
To The Commission: 
Your advice is requested as to whether or not I may accept a gift 
that was given to me by the Council of Black Administrators (COBA) 
of the Los Angeles Unified School District. 
I recently attended a 
function sponsored by COBA and was given a $100 traveler's 
check 
as an expression of appreciation for my leadership on issues they 
believe to be important. 
This gift was in no way connected with a speech or presentation or 
other action on my part. 
It was simply an unsolicited expression 
of appreciation by COBA. 
Assuming that this gift is properly disclosed on my Conflict of 
Interest forms, 
am 
I 
permitted by law or your regulations to 
accept the gift, or must I return it? 
Your written advice on this matter would be most appreciated. 
Should you have any questions regarding this, please feel free to 
contact me. 
Board Member 
RW:ew 
cc: 
Richard K. Mason,

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 18, 1989 
Rita Walters 
Los Angeles School Board Member 
P.O. Box 3307 
Los Angeles, CA 
90051 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-417 
Dear Ms. Walters: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on July 14, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660
